★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.83 3.89 3.81 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.44 0.68 0.43 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.82 0.86 0.81 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.58 2.34 2.56 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.26 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.41
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.25
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.39
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-12-19 14 12 14 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-08-10 7 7 7 64 1 64
Total weighted health score
67.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (22)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

21 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2025-08-06 2025-09-20
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-08-06 2025-09-20
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-12-19 2025-02-02
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-08-10 2023-09-28
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2023-08-10 2023-09-28
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. G 2023-08-10 2023-09-28
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-08-10 2023-09-28
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-08-10 2023-09-28
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-08-10 2023-09-28
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-08-10 2023-09-28
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2022-02-03 2022-03-18
